mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower future cash flows and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production and housing starts indicates supply chain bottlenecks colliding with weak demand, forcing margin compression and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO * HOUST) shows no statistically significant predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated while the federal funds rate stays below the neutral rate, real rates turn negative, prompting a rotation into equities and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-inflation periods do not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 0.5% per month, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real consumer credit balances reflect expanding household purchasing power and confidence, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The monthly change in RSAFS shows no monotonic relationship with 5-day forward SPX returns across a 10-year backtest, or the sign of the coefficient flips in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production below its long-term trend signals weakening manufacturing demand, leading to inventory buildups and margin pressure that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO breaches the 105.0 threshold, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong employment growth and rising consumer credit balances indicate a self-reinforcing economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant during market stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader economic activity and construction-related corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon when building permits accelerate.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive skew when HOUST exceeds 1300.0, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward validation.
